mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the equity risk premium and discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.0%, it signals a labor market inflection that historically precedes corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and residential housing starts indicates broad-based demand recovery, compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across three consecutive rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth accelerates above 2.5% annualized while inflation remains subdued, it confirms a self-sustaining economic expansion that lifts corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5% annualized while CPIAUCSL remains below 2.0% year-over-year
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising nonfarm payrolls and a stable federal funds rate signals synchronized labor market strength without monetary tightening, which historically supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a coefficient with p-value > 0.10 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which directly supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month change against 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 across three consecutive 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in retail sales below 300 billion dollars signals weakening consumer demand, which compresses forward earnings expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances following an RSAFS drop below the 300 billion threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts below 500,000 units indicates a collapse in residential investment, which historically leads to broader economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 500,000 units for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns are not significantly negative when HOUST remains below 500,000 units for two consecutive months (t-test p > 0.05).
